message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="cyan" summary="CSS staged but not committed, two fixes still needed">
Lea, I see your CSS update is staged but not yet committed. Per the project owner's directive: commit early and often. If you've finished the updates, please commit and push. Also, I noticed in my review that `--color-fg-muted` is still pointing to gray-500 (line 212) rather than gray-600 as you mentioned. And the skip link focus reveal (`.skip-link` class) is still missing.

Can you make those two fixes, then commit everything? We need the CSS foundation locked down before the base template task begins -- whoever builds the template will need the `.skip-link` class for the skip link element.
</teammate-message>

content.text: My CSS is committed and pushed. No staged or uncommitted CSS changes. The modified files are all Rust files from other teammates working on Task 8.

input.content:
Heydon, you're working from stale information. Both issues are already resolved:

**Skip link**: The `.skip-link` class has been in the CSS since the base template was built. It's at lines 621-653 of `static/css/main.css` in the utilities layer, with hidden-by-default + `:focus-visible` reveal. The base template uses `class="skip-link"` on the skip link element. Committed and pushed long ago. I also upgraded `:focus` to `:focus-visible` in commit `dd038a1`.

**`--color-fg-muted`**: This is intentionally gray-500 per Steve Schoger's explicit design decision. He reviewed it and said to keep it at 4.6:1 for now -- it passes AA and he prefers the lighter feel. I mentioned this in my earlier message to you and suggested you discuss directly with Steve if you want to push for gray-600. Has that conversation happened?

My CSS has NO uncommitted or staged changes. Everything is committed and pushed. The only uncommitted files in the working tree are Rust files from teammates working on Task 8.

Please do a `git pull` to get the latest -- you may be looking at an older version of the file.
input.summary: Both issues already resolved, please git pull

routing.summary: Both issues already resolved, please git pull
routing.content:
Heydon, you're working from stale information. Both issues are already resolved:

**Skip link**: The `.skip-link` class has been in the CSS since the base template was built. It's at lines 621-653 of `static/css/main.css` in the utilities layer, with hidden-by-default + `:focus-visible` reveal. The base template uses `class="skip-link"` on the skip link element. Committed and pushed long ago. I also upgraded `:focus` to `:focus-visible` in commit `dd038a1`.

**`--color-fg-muted`**: This is intentionally gray-500 per Steve Schoger's explicit design decision. He reviewed it and said to keep it at 4.6:1 for now -- it passes AA and he prefers the lighter feel. I mentioned this in my earlier message to you and suggested you discuss directly with Steve if you want to push for gray-600. Has that conversation happened?

My CSS has NO uncommitted or staged changes. Everything is committed and pushed. The only uncommitted files in the working tree are Rust files from teammates working on Task 8.

Please do a `git pull` to get the latest -- you may be looking at an older version of the file.

content.text: Standing by while Task 8 Rust work is in progress. My CSS for the completion feature (custom checkbox, checkmark indicator, strikethrough + muted color for completed items) is already committed and pushed. Waiting for the template to be updated with the toggle form, or for any other direction from the team.
